primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI Stage 3) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates sepsis risk per Sepsis-3 definitions with lactate 4.2 mmol/L, WBC 14.2 K/uL, and AKI Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL). Platelets remain <100 K/uL across three measurements (88-95 K/uL), meeting SOFA coagulopathy criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL suggest possible hepatic involvement and anemia. Creatinine 8.9 mg/dL was excluded as outlier due to 6.5 mg/dL rise in 6 hours, likely measurement error.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (excluded outlier) would meet KDIGO Stage 3 (≥3x baseline or ≥4 mg/dL)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring (platelets <150 K/uL = 1 point)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis resuscitation bundle per Sepsis-3 guidelines
Source control investigation for suspected infection
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and creatinine trends
Consider platelet transfusion if bleeding risk present (platelets <50 K/uL)
Reassess creatinine measurement for accuracy after outlier exclusion
doctor_handoff: Confirm infection source, assess for septic shock criteria (vasopressor requirements), and evaluate for AKI reversal strategies. Review coagulation profile for bleeding risk.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
Lactate elevation to 4.2 mmol/L, platelets decline to 95 K/uL, AKI Stage 1 (creatinine 2.1 mg/dL)
Platelets 88 K/uL, AKI Stage 3 (excluded creatinine 8.9 mg/dL as outlier)

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
